reasoning: A has the cleanest reasoning: it explicitly catches the question-substitution trap (P(STEM) unconditional vs the two-way conditional split), names the load-bearing inputs in a sensitivity table, and uses an outside-view check without circularity, with the tightest yet honest ranges. B brings the strongest empirical grounding (BLS OEWS 6,170 national/360 Chicago metro; named Crist|Kolder 2025 figures), but it has an internal inconsistency in (a) where a city-proper headline of 100 and a metro range of 250-450 sit awkwardly against its own cited metro anchor of 360, and it surfaces fewer cognitive traps. C is well-structured with the most defensible wide range (60-250) and good sector-mix reasoning, but adds the least novel evidence and stays closest to generic Fermi, so it ranks third.
